nodes.label: AI as research assistant for complex legal topics
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable for nuanced legal questions
nodes.grounding_span: for more complex topics I would use AI

nodes.label: AI as drafting and refinement tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as poor initiator of tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I don't find AI to be very good at starting tasks, but it is good at helping you refine something once it is already started

nodes.label: AI as quality checker and confidence booster
nodes.label_negative: AI as source of unreliable information requiring verification
nodes.grounding_span: I also find it really useful to check over my work and to point out if something doesn't make sense

nodes.label: AI as tutor for skill development
nodes.label_negative: AI as crutch leading to over-reliance
nodes.grounding_span: I would like to see it as a tutor who is helping me through my work more than an assistant who is actually doing all of the work for me

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner requiring oversight
nodes.label_negative: AI as autonomous delegate without supervision
nodes.grounding_span: There is nothing that I would feel completely happy delegating to AI without some form of oversight

nodes.label: AI as source of external verification
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable source requiring fact-checking
nodes.grounding_span: I would always externally verify what the AI said as it's answers can be a little unreliable

nodes.label: Professional competence and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: I am still being thorough with my job

nodes.label: Personal autonomy and skill development
nodes.grounding_span: I would like to learn how to do these things myself first so that I am confident when reviewing the AI's work

nodes.label: Efficiency and productivity
nodes.grounding_span: I am also getting work done quickly

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for compiling rough work into client-ready summary
nodes.grounding_span: the model compiled it nicely into a summary which I was able to send to my boss and then to our client. That was a satisfying one

nodes.label: Frustration with AI providing incorrect legal sources
nodes.grounding_span: the AI I was using kept on giving me 'examples' of where a structure was defined, including specific sections in statute, however all of these were wrong

nodes.label: Cautious trust in AI for bounded tasks with verification
nodes.grounding_span: I would always externally verify what the AI said as it's answers can be a little unreliable

nodes.label: Aspirational openness to AI drafting documents after learning
nodes.grounding_span: If I can give details to an AI to draft documents then that would be great

nodes.label: Systematic verification and source-checking
nodes.grounding_span: I ask the AI to provide a source for each claim and then I will go and view the webpage to check that it actually says what the AI claims it says

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through back-and-forth interaction
nodes.grounding_span: I often start something, ask for the AI's opinion, refine what I am doing, and then go back and forth until I have a refined version that I am happy with
